events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes structural welding done without qualified WPS (Welding Procedure Specification) parameters, and the work has already been through its testing/inspection step. AWS D1.1 requires that welding be performed using qualified WPS. Welding without qualified WPS parameters is a serious non-compliance issue.

Key points:
- Welding without qualified WPS parameters violates AWS D1.1
- Work is at tested/inspected stage
- This is a fail - non-compliant work
- Risk is high/critical because structural welds without qualified parameters could fail
- Component condition: installed-defective, non-compliant
- The welds may need to be cut out and re-welded with proper WPS

Let me construct the JSON.

    "Plan removal and re-welding of affected structural connections using qualified WPS",
    "Re-inspect and re-test reworked welds per AWS D1.1 inspection requirements"
  ],
  "rationale": "AWS D1.1 requires that all welding be performed in accordance with a qualified Welding Procedure Specification. Welding without qualified WPS parameters means the essential variables (amperage, voltage, travel speed, preheat, etc.) were not controlled to a proven, qualified range. The resulting welds cannot be reliably accepted even if visual or NDT inspection passes, because mechanical and metallurgical properties are unverified. This is a critical non-compliance on structural connections where failure could endanger life safety and structural integrity.",
  "references": [
    "AWS D1.1 Clause 5 - Qualification",
    "AWS D1.1 Clause 6 - Welding Procedure Specification (WPS) requirements",
    "AWS D1.1 Clause 7 - Inspection"
